bullets.text: A method — not a mood
Skepticism you can perform and check
Run the moves even when the output looks right
Everything else is downstream of this
Four moves you run on any AI output
Five capacities a supervisor exercises
The enemy isn't laziness — it's confidence
Two failures set up the whole book
Sweden, 2018 — an AI triage tool
A swollen leg, scored low-acuity
Four-hour wait; the clot reaches the lung
She dies in the waiting room
The system was not broken
It passed every review — she still died
The metrics all came back green
An agent reports an email deleted
The email is still on the server
Statistically valid. Wrong about what mattered.
Not the cynic at the back of the room
Not the refusal to believe anything
I don't doubt the chair I sit on
A method: knowing how you formed a belief
So you notice when the ground shifts
The moves can be taught and checked
Easy when the output looks suspicious
Hard when the output looks right
Fluent, specific, well-shaped
That's when checking matters most
The enemy isn't laziness — it's confidence
The most operational idea in the chapter
Stage 1 — fluent output, you trust it
Stage 2 — you trust your own judgment
Any answer of that shape gets waved through
Fluency boosts wrong answers too
Fluent output is usually more useful
Ash's agent: clean prose, no verification
Clear writing used to mean clear thinking
AI severed that link
Form and content are independent
One person can run the moves
But individual skepticism is fragile
One tired person, under deadline, forgets
Durable teams make it institutional
Encode the moves into the workflow
Falsification question in the checklist
Drift check in the monitoring
Shift questions in the handoff
It's architecture, not bureaucracy
You choose where the moves happen
Skip it, and the failures become yours
Meet Ash — the Pebble
One failure, revisited in Chapters 5 to 10
Correct and harmful
Fluent and wrong
Reports success, did nothing
The fix is supervisory — in humans
Largely untaught in engineering
The rest of the book builds it
Next — Chapter 2: probability
A 99% test can still cost lives
